title: oxalis bifida
intro: Oxalis bifida, also known as the two-toothed wood sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family, Oxalidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anthelmintic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat conditions such as intestinal worms, wounds, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Oxalis bifida include its ability to support digestive health, boost the immune system, and reduce inflammation. Oxalis bifida is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be used in tincture form to harness its medicinal properties.
